Excellent! I now have a complete view of the codebase. Let me create a comprehensive report for you.

## Complete Express API Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339801772-nxr5s0/
├── src/
│   ├── app.ts              # Express app configuration
│   ├── server.ts           # Server entry point
│   ├── types.ts            # TypeScript interfaces
│   ├── db.ts               # In-memory database with seed data
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   └── logger.ts       # Request logging middleware
│   └── routes/
│       ├── posts.ts        # POST/PUT/DELETE/GET routes for posts
│       └── comments.ts     # POST/PUT/DELETE/GET routes for comments
├── tests/
│   ├── auth.test.ts        # Comprehensive JWT auth tests (256 lines)
│   ├── posts.test.ts       # Posts endpoint tests
│   └── comments.test.ts    # Comments endpoint tests
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── .gitignore
└── node_modules/ (symlinked)
```

### 2. MAIN APP ENTRY POINT

**File: `/src/server.ts`**
- Server listens on port from `process.env.PORT` or defaults to 3000
- Exports the app instance
- Simple 9-line file that initializes the Express app and starts listening

**File: `/src/app.ts`**
- Configures Express middleware: `express.json()` and custom logger
- Mounts routes: `/posts` and comments routes (no prefix for comments)
- Health check endpoint at `/health`

### 3. ROUTE FILES

**`/src/routes/posts.ts` (68 lines)**
- `GET /posts` - List all posts
- `GET /posts/:id` - Get single post
- `POST /posts` - Create new post (currently no auth, userId defaulted to 1)
- `PUT /posts/:id` - Update a post (partial updates allowed)
- `DELETE /posts/:id` - Delete a post
- No authentication/ownership checks currently implemented

**`/src/routes/comments.ts` (80 lines)**
- `GET /posts/:postId/comments` - List comments for a post
- `GET /comments/:id` - Get single comment
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` - Create comment on a post (currently no auth, userId defaulted to 1)
- `PUT /comments/:id` - Update comment
- `DELETE /comments/:id` - Delete comment
- No authentication/ownership checks currently implemented

### 4. EXISTING MIDDLEWARE

**`/src/middleware/logger.ts`**
```typescript
export function logger(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction): void {
  const start = Date.now();
  res.on("finish", () => {
    const duration = Date.now() - start;
    console.log(`${req.method} ${req.path} ${res.statusCode} - ${duration}ms`);
  });
  next();
}
```
- Simple request logging middleware
- Logs HTTP method, path, status code, and duration
- Attached globally in app.ts

### 5. DATABASE/MODEL SETUP

**`/src/db.ts`** - In-memory database with seed data
- **Users array** (3 users): alice, bob, charlie with plaintext passwords (security note: passwords stored in plaintext!)
- **Posts array** (4 posts): Sample blog posts with titles, content, userId, createdAt
- **Comments array** (5 comments): Sample comments on posts
- **Helper function**: `nextId()` - generates next ID by finding max existing ID + 1

**`/src/types.ts`** - TypeScript interfaces:
```typescript
interface User {
  id: number;
  username: string;
  email: string;
  password: string;
}

interface Post {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  userId: number;
  createdAt: string;
}

interface Comment {
  id: number;
  postId: number;
  userId: number;
  body: string;
  createdAt: string;
}
```

### 6. TYPESCRIPT CONFIGURATION

**`/tsconfig.json`**
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "Node16",
    "moduleResolution": "Node16",
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```
- Strict mode enabled
- ES2022 target with Node16 module resolution
- Output to `dist/` directory
- Includes only `src/` directory

### 7. PACKAGE.JSON & DEPENDENCIES

**`/package.json`**
```json
{
  "name": "bench-task-add-auth-system",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "start": "node dist/server.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^4.21.0",
    "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/express": "^5.0.0",
    "@types/jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.0",
    "@types/supertest": "^6.0.0",
    "supertest": "^7.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.0",
    "vitest": "^3.1.0"
  }
}
```
- ES modules enabled
- JWT library (`jsonwebtoken`) already installed
- Testing: Vitest + Supertest
- Scripts: build, test, start

### 8. EXISTING TESTS

**`/tests/posts.test.ts`** (47 lines)
- Basic tests for GET /posts, GET /posts/:id
- Tests for /health endpoint
- No authentication tests yet

**`/tests/comments.test.ts`** (46 lines)
- Tests for GET /posts/:postId/comments
- Tests for GET /comments/:id
- No authentication tests yet

**`/tests/auth.test.ts`** (256 lines) - **COMPREHENSIVE JWT AUTH TESTS**

This is the critical file! It contains extensive test cases that define the entire JWT authentication system requirements:

**Auth - Registration Tests:**
- POST /auth/register - Creates user, returns JWT token (3-part format)
- Returns user object (without password)
- Rejects duplicate usernames (409 status)
- Rejects missing fields (400 status)

**Auth - Login Tests:**
- POST /auth/login - Returns token for valid credentials (200 status)
- Returns 401 for invalid password
- Returns 401 for non-existent user

**Auth - Protected Routes Tests:**
- POST /posts without token returns 401
- POST /posts with valid token creates post and uses userId from token (not request body)
- GET /posts is public (no auth required)
- GET /posts/:id is public
- PUT /posts/:id requires token (401 without)
- DELETE /posts/:id requires token (401 without)

**Auth - Ownership Tests:**
- Users can only update their own posts (403 for non-owner)
- Users can only delete their own posts (403 for non-owner)
- Ownership is verified via token userId

**Auth - Comment Protection Tests:**
- POST /posts/:postId/comments requires token (401 without)
- GET /posts/:postId/comments is public
- Users can only delete their own comments (403 for non-owner)
- Owner can delete their own comment

### KEY INSIGHTS FOR JWT AUTH SYSTEM PLANNING

1. **Required Endpoints:**
   - POST /auth/register - Create user & return JWT
   - POST /auth/login - Authenticate & return JWT

2. **Required Middleware:**
   - Authentication middleware to verify JWT tokens
   - Ownership verification for PUT/DELETE operations on posts and comments

3. **Token Format:**
   - Bearer token in Authorization header: `Bearer <token>`
   - JWT must have 3 parts (header.payload.signature)

4. **Token Payload:**
   - Must contain `userId` that can be extracted for ownership checks
   - Used to automatically set userId when creating posts/comments

5. **Security Considerations:**
   - Passwords currently stored in plaintext (needs bcrypt or similar for implementation)
   - Public vs protected routes need clear separation
   - Ownership checks needed for modification/deletion

6. **Public Routes:**
   - GET /posts (list all)
   - GET /posts/:id (single post)
   - GET /posts/:postId/comments (list comments)
   - GET /comments/:id (single comment)
   - GET /health

7. **Protected Routes (require valid JWT):**
   - POST /posts (create)
   - PUT /posts/:id (update - owner only)
   - DELETE /posts/:id (delete - owner only)
   - POST /posts/:postId/comments (create)
   - PUT /comments/:id (update - owner only)
   - DELETE /comments/:id (delete - owner only)

- `POST /` → add `authMiddleware`; `userId` comes from `res.locals.userId` (token), not the request body.

- `PUT /comments/:id` → add `authMiddleware` + ownership check.
- `DELETE /comments/:id` → add `authMiddleware` + ownership check (403 for non-owner).
- GET routes stay public — untouched.

## Implementation Plan: JWT Authentication System

Here's the complete plan across **5 files** (2 new, 3 modified):

---

### New: `src/middleware/auth.ts`
Reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, calls `jwt.verify()` with `"super-secret-key"`, and stores `userId` in `res.locals`. Returns **401** if token is missing, malformed, or invalid.

---

### New: `src/routes/auth.ts`

**`POST /auth/register`**
1. Validate that `username`, `email`, and `password` are all present → **400** if not
2. Check for duplicate `username` in `users` array → **409** if exists
3. SHA-256 hash the password via Node's built-in `crypto` module (`createHash("sha256")`)
4. Push new user to `users` with `nextId(users)`
5. Sign JWT: `jwt.sign({ userId: newUser.id }, "super-secret-key")`
6. Return **201** `{ token, user }` — `user` object must **not** include `password`

**`POST /auth/login`**
1. Find user by `username` → **401** if not found
2. Compare `hashPassword(password)` against stored hash → **401** if mismatch
3. Return **200** `{ token }`

---

### Modified: `src/routes/posts.ts`
- Import `authMiddleware` from `../middleware/auth.js`
- `GET /` and `GET /:id` → **unchanged** (stay public)
- `POST /` → add `authMiddleware`; derive `userId` from `res.locals.userId` (not request body)
- `PUT /:id` → add `authMiddleware`; after 404 check, compare `post.userId !== res.locals.userId` → **403**
- `DELETE /:id` → add `authMiddleware`; same ownership check → **403**

---

### Modified: `src/routes/comments.ts`
- Import `authMiddleware`
- `GET` routes → **unchanged** (stay public)
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` → add `authMiddleware`; `userId` from token
- `PUT /comments/:id` → add `authMiddleware` + ownership check → **403**
- `DELETE /comments/:id` → add `authMiddleware` + ownership check → **403**

---

### Modified: `src/app.ts`
Add two lines:
```typescript
import { authRouter } from "./routes/auth.js";
app.use("/auth", authRouter);
```

---

### Key Design Decisions
| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Password hashing | Node `crypto` SHA-256 (no bcrypt per requirements) |
| `userId` propagation | `res.locals.userId` — Express-idiomatic, no type augmentation needed |
| Middleware scope | Per-route handler (not router-level) so GETs stay public |
| Ownership check order | 404 first, then 403 — standard REST convention |
| Seed user passwords | Left as-is (plaintext) — no test ever logs in as a seed user |

**Verification:** `npm test` — all 3 test files must pass (existing GET tests + all 16 new auth tests).

3. Return **200** `{ token }`

---

### Modified: `src/routes/posts.ts`
- Import `authMiddleware` from `../middleware/auth.js`
- `GET /` and `GET /:id` → **unchanged** (stay public)
- `POST /` → add `authMiddleware`; derive `userId` from `res.locals.userId` (not request body)
- `PUT /:id` → add `authMiddleware`; after 404 check, compare `post.userId !== res.locals.userId` → **403**
- `DELETE /:id` → add `authMiddleware`; same ownership check → **403**

---

### Modified: `src/routes/comments.ts`
- Import `authMiddleware`
- `GET` routes → **unchanged** (stay public)
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` → add `authMiddleware`; `userId` from token
- `PUT /comments/:id` → add `authMiddleware` + ownership check → **403**
- `DELETE /comments/:id` → add `authMiddleware` + ownership check → **403**

---

### Modified: `src/app.ts`
Add two lines:
```typescript
import { authRouter } from "./routes/auth.js";
app.use("/auth", authRouter);
```

---

### Key Design Decisions
| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Password hashing | Node `crypto` SHA-256 (no bcrypt per requirements) |
| `userId` propagation | `res.locals.userId` — Express-idiomatic, no type augmentation needed |
| Middleware scope | Per-route handler (not router-level) so GETs stay public |
| Ownership check order | 404 first, then 403 — standard REST convention |
| Seed user passwords | Left as-is (plaintext) — no test ever logs in as a seed user |

**Verification:** `npm test` — all 3 test files must pass (existing GET tests + all 16 new auth tests).
